outcome_contract.why: Staff identity is caller-controlled at the Prime API layer â€” any holder of the gateway token can attribute actions to any staff UID, including audit records, broadcast sends, and message attributions. For broadcast specifically it is a direct-harm risk (messages to all active guests attributed to wrong person).
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: After this change, the actor UID recorded at the Prime layer is cryptographically bound to a short-lived signed claims header verified with a separate signing secret. A caller with only the gateway token cannot forge a different UID. The broadcast send path additionally requires the caller to hold the `owner` or `admin` role (exact allowlist, not hierarchical).

engineering_coverage.applicable: Closes UID forgery for all 8 endpoints; broadcast role gate enforced; replay mitigated by `iat` window; signing secret independent of gateway token
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Highest assurance (Firebase-verified JWT); but exposes ID token in transit
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: HMAC-SHA256 with separate secret is sufficient for threat model; scope of protection documented (gateway-token leakage, not full reception compromise)

engineering_coverage.applicable: All actorUid values in D1 going forward are verifiably bound; log HMAC failures with reason
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Same; ID token gives richer identity context
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Add structured log entries for HMAC rejection (reason: missing, invalid-sig, expired)

engineering_coverage.applicable: Unit-testable: HMAC sign/verify utilities pure functions; role gate unit-testable; extend `review-threads.test.ts` + `staff-initiate-thread.test.ts` + new tests for `review-campaign` POST/PUT
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Jest node env sufficient; no E2E needed for security path

engineering_coverage.applicable: New `x-prime-actor-claims` header replaces `x-prime-actor-uid`; D1 schema unchanged; rollout compat via parallel header acceptance
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Same contract surface; ID token header replaces gateway header
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Backward-compat: non-broadcast endpoints accept both headers during compat window; broadcast endpoints require signed claims immediately

engineering_coverage.applicable: One `crypto.subtle.verify` call per request (~0.1ms on CF Workers); negligible
Step 1: Prime accepts both headers (broadcast 3 endpoints: signed-only, non-broadcast 5 endpoints: signed or plain); Step 2: Reception sends signed headers; Step 3: remove plain-UID fallback
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Rollback after Reception starts sending signed claims: Prime's `\

stage_payload.decision_summary: The Prime messaging staff API endpoints (8 mutation endpoints) authenticate callers via a shared static gateway token but trust the caller-supplied `x-prime-actor-uid` header to identify which staff member is acting. The analysis must decide: 1. **How to bind actor identity at the Prime layer** so that a gateway-token holder cannot forge a different UID. 2. **How to enforce a broadcast role gate** on the two broadcast-facing endpoints (`staff-initiate-thread`, `review-campaign-send`) without introducing full Firebase token verification on Prime's CF Workers runtime. 3. **How to sequence the ro
goals: Actor UID recorded in Prime D1 is cryptographically bound to a verified claims payload.
Broadcast initiation requires the `owner` or `admin` role (exact flat-set allowlist).
All 8 mutation endpoints covered; no endpoint left unhardened.
No full auth rewrite; no new infrastructure; no Firebase Admin SDK on Prime.
non_goals: Per-request Firebase ID token verification on Prime (adds JWKS network dependency).
Rebuilding multi-staff auth for Prime (single PIN today).
Retroactive correction of existing audit records with potentially forged UIDs.
constraints: Prime runs as Cloudflare Pages Functions; only WebCrypto available (no Node.js crypto, no Firebase Admin SDK).
assumptions: A new `PRIME_ACTOR_CLAIMS_SECRET` env var (distinct from the gateway token) provisioned in both reception and Prime is the minimum additional configuration required.
HMAC-SHA256 via `crypto.subtle` is available and performant on both CF Workers and Next.js edge.

evaluation_criteria.Criterion: Closes C-04 UID forgery for all 8 endpoints
Does not use gateway token as signing key
Closes L-04 broadcast role gate immediately (no compat window)

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: Non-broadcast degraded gracefully if reception not yet deployed
Both services must stay in sync

options_considered.Description: New `PRIME_ACTOR_CLAIMS_SECRET` env var; reception signs `{uid, roles, iat}` with HMAC-SHA256; Prime verifies and extracts claims
options_considered.Upside: No new infrastructure; WebCrypto on both runtimes; closes UID forgery and enables role gate; 1 new env var
options_considered.Downside: Requires coordinated deployment of both reception and Prime; compat window for non-broadcast only
options_considered.Key risks: Secret must not equal gateway token; replay attack window (mitigated by timestamp)

options_considered.Option: B â€” Forward Firebase ID token; Prime verifies via JWKS
options_considered.Description: Reception forwards Firebase ID token in `x-prime-actor-id-token` header; Prime fetches Google JWKS and verifies JWT
options_considered.Upside: Full Firebase auth verification; most rigorous
options_considered.Downside: Prime must fetch JWKS on every request (cold-start latency, JWKS rotation complexity); no role information in ID token without custom claims; significantly higher complexity
options_considered.Key risks: JWKS cache invalidation; cold-start latency spike; ID token forwarding increases attack surface if intercepted
options_considered.Viable?: No â€” disproportionate complexity for this deployment size

options_considered.Option: C â€” Server-side tokenâ†’UID mapping in D1 or KV
options_considered.Description: Pre-register gateway-tokenâ†’{uid, roles} mapping in Prime's D1 or KV
options_considered.Downside: New admin infrastructure to manage; D1/KV becomes auth state; every new staff member requires a D1/KV update; no standard interface
options_considered.Key risks: Operational burden; stale mapping risk; new attack surface (KV injection)
options_considered.Viable?: No â€” adds infrastructure complexity without clear gain over Option A

options_considered.Option: D â€” Cloudflare Access emailâ†’UID lookup
options_considered.Downside: Only works when CF Access is active (not static-token path); email is not a UID; requires a separate emailâ†’UID lookup against RTDB; static-token callers (reception) bypass CF Access
options_considered.Key risks: Only partial coverage; inconsistent with static-token callers
options_considered.Viable?: No â€” does not cover the reception caller path

chosen_approach.Recommendation: Option A â€” Separate-secret HMAC claims signing/verification using a new `PRIME_ACTOR_CLAIMS_SECRET` env var, independent of the gateway token.
chosen_approach.**Broadcast role gate applies to 3 endpo: `staff-initiate-thread`, `review-campaign-send`, AND `review-thread-send`. `review-thread-send` internally calls `isWholeHostelBroadcastThread` and handles the whole-hostel broadcast path when no campaign record exists yet. All 3 must enforce the role gate with no compat window.
chosen_approach.Rollback from a state where Reception ha: Prime's legacy `|| 'prime-owner'` fallback means rollback collapses actor attribution to `prime-owner` (not a restore of the pre-fix plain-UID state). This is acceptable for rollback â€” audit records will show `prime-owner` until re-deployment. Document this in the rollback runbook.

rejected_approaches: Option B (Firebase ID token + JWKS) â€” JWKS cold-start latency (50â€“200ms per request), key rotation complexity, and no standard role claim in Firebase ID tokens make this disproportionately complex for a 2-staff deployment. Revisit only if Prime scales to an untrusted multi-caller environment.

Option C (server-side tokenâ†’UID mapping in D1/KV) â€” Requires new admin infrastructure, operational burden for every new staff member, and introduces a new writable attack surface. No advantages over Option A.

Option D (CF Access emailâ†’UID) â€” Only covers CF Access sessions; the reception static-token caller bypasses CF Access entirely. Partial coverage is worse than the current state because it creates a false sense of security.

areas.label: Reception auth
areas.current: `requireStaffAuth` validates Firebase ID token, extracts `uid` and `roles`
areas.trigger: Staff POST to reception inbox API
areas.future: Same verification; additionally: `buildPrimeActorHeaders` signs `{uid, roles, iat}` with `PRIME_ACTOR_CLAIMS_SECRET` â†’ sends `x-prime-actor-claims: <b64url-payload>.<b64url-sig>` header
areas.steady: Firebase ID token verification, role lookup against RTDB

areas.label: Prime gateway enforcement
areas.current: `enforceStaffOwnerApiGate` validates static token or CF Access
areas.trigger: Any request to Prime staff API
areas.future: Same gateway check (unchanged); new `resolveActorClaims(request, env)` helper called after gate passes on mutation endpoints; verifies HMAC, checks timestamp, returns `{ uid, roles }` or rejects with 401

areas.label: Broadcast role gate (new)
areas.current: Absent â€” any gateway-token caller can broadcast
areas.seams: roles.includes('admin')`; return 403 if role not present. (`review-thread-send` handles whole-hostel broadcast when no campaign exists â€” confirmed via `isWholeHostelBroadcastThread` check in `prime-review-send.ts`)

areas.label: Non-broadcast mutation endpoints (6)
areas.current: UID from caller header, no verification
areas.future: `resolveActorClaims` called; accepts signed claims or (during compat window) plain `x-prime-actor-uid`; compat window removed within 1 sprint
areas.seams: Compat window must have hard-gate removal tracked as blocking TASK

areas.label: Audit / D1 records
areas.current: `actorUid` stored as-is; may contain forged values
areas.future: Going forward: all `actorUid` values in D1 are verifiably bound to a signed claims payload
areas.steady: Pre-fix historical records with potentially forged UIDs remain as-is (not retroactively corrected)
areas.seams: Pre-fix records flaggable by timestamp if audit investigation requires
